skill.name: pricing-strategy
skill.description: Helps teams make pricing, packaging, and monetization decisions using SaaS pricing frameworks, research methods, and tier design guidance.
skill.summary: Design pricing models, packaging tiers, and monetization plans for SaaS and digital products.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command, sensitive data, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, SaaS terms such as SAML, and ordinary pricing language. No executable code, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences around diagrams, sample survey output, tier tables, and customer email copy. They do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands or execute Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are visible Markdown fence delimiters around static business examples. No command syntax, interpreter directive, or execution workflow is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SAML Flagged as Windows SAM Database
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static scanner matched the substring SAM inside SAML. The content discusses enterprise single sign-on features, not Windows credential stores.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both occurrences are SSO/SAML feature references in pricing tier guidance. There is no mention of Windows files, registry paths, credential extraction, or dumping behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain pricing strategy prose such as description, design, descriptions, and includes. They do not reference DES, MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is business guidance and tier descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, encryption workflow, or security implementation appears at these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Business Context
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged terms are used for SaaS pricing concepts, including support levels, full access trials, churn rate, API access, and platform fees. No system or network probing is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding sections are freemium, trial, support, churn, and enterprise pricing guidance. There are no host discovery, port scanning, network mapping, or command examples.

content.value_statement: Pricing decisions often fail when teams guess at value, tiers, or willingness to pay. This skill provides structured frameworks for research, packaging, price points, and rollout planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides value-based pricing decisions using customer value, alternatives, and cost floors.
Explains Van Westendorp, MaxDiff, and willingness-to-pay research methods.
Helps design good-better-best tier structures for SaaS products.
Compares freemium, free trial, reverse trial, and hybrid entry models.
Provides guidance for price increases, grandfathering, and customer communication.
Outlines enterprise pricing options such as custom contracts and platform fees.
limitations: It does not collect survey responses or run statistical analysis by itself.
It cannot verify competitor pricing unless the user supplies current data.
It provides strategy guidance, not legal or financial advice.
It cannot predict conversion or churn outcomes with certainty.

use_cases.title: Set First Pricing for a New SaaS Product
use_cases.description: Create initial tiers, value metrics, and price ranges before launch.

use_cases.title: Improve Packaging for Growth
use_cases.description: Restructure features, limits, and upgrade triggers across pricing tiers.

use_cases.title: Plan a Price Increase
use_cases.description: Assess timing, customer impact, grandfathering options, and communication steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Current Pricing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our current pricing and suggest improvements. Product: [describe product]. Customers: [segments]. Current tiers: [tiers and prices]. Goals: [growth, revenue, or profit].

prompt_templates.title: Design New Pricing Tiers
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a good-better-best pricing structure for [product]. Include target users, feature gates, usage limits, price ranges, and upgrade triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Pricing Research Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a pricing research method for [product]. Compare Van Westendorp, MaxDiff, and willingness-to-pay interviews for our situation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Pricing Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pricing migration plan for moving from [old model] to [new model]. Include risks, grandfathering, metrics, messaging, and rollout sequence.

output_examples.input: We have one flat $49 plan and want to add tiers.
output: A Starter tier should reduce adoption friction for small teams.
A Pro tier should anchor the core value and hold the best upgrade path.
A Business tier should gate admin, security, support, and higher limits.

output_examples.input: We want to test whether our price is too low.
output: Start with customer interviews and willingness-to-pay questions.
Compare results against competitor alternatives and perceived value.
Use new-customer pricing first before changing existing customer plans.

output_examples.input: Should we use freemium or a free trial?
output: Use freemium when free users create distribution or network value.
Use a trial when customers need full access to experience value.
Consider a reverse trial if premium value is hard to explain upfront.

best_practices: Start with customer value and alternatives before setting price points.
Use clear tier differences based on features, usage, support, or security needs.
Track conversion, revenue, churn, and customer feedback after pricing changes.
anti_patterns: Do not copy competitor prices without understanding your own value.
Do not create many tiers when three clear tiers would work better.
Do not raise prices for existing customers without a communication plan.

faq.question: Can this skill create a complete pricing page?
faq.answer: It can define tier structure, messaging points, and page content guidance. It does not design a full visual page.

faq.question: Does it support usage-based pricing?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps evaluate value metrics, usage thresholds, platform fees, and hybrid models.

faq.question: Can it analyze Van Westendorp survey results?
faq.answer: It explains how to interpret results and recommended ranges. The user must provide the survey data.

faq.question: Is it only for SaaS companies?
faq.answer: It focuses on SaaS, but many frameworks apply to marketplaces, services, and digital products.

faq.question: Can it recommend exact prices?
faq.answer: It can suggest ranges and reasoning. Exact prices need market data, customer research, and business constraints.

faq.question: Does it cover enterprise pricing?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers contact-sales tiers, custom contracts, support levels, security features, and platform fees.
